history.qwen_observation: The build failed on JDK 8 because maven-compiler-plugin 3.8.0 passed the --release flag to javac, which is unsupported in JDK 8.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading maven-compiler-plugin to 3.11.0 and explicitly aligning maven.compiler.release and java.version properties to 8 ensures the compiler plugin does not pass unsupported flags to the JDK 8 javac, allowing the initial step to succeed while preserving the rest of the migration trajectory.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed because maven.compiler.release was set to 8 while running on JDK 8, which does not support the --release compiler flag introduced in JDK 9.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the maven.compiler.release property change and the premature compiler plugin bump from the initial step prevents the JDK 8 compiler from receiving unsupported flags. Subsequent migration steps will safely configure the compiler plugin and release flags as the JDK version increases to 11, 17, and 21.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed on JDK 11 with NoClassDefFoundError: javax/xml/bind/JAXBException because JAXB modules were removed from the JDK in Java 11 and are not currently declared as dependencies.
history.qwen_rationale: Explicitly adding the javax.xml.bind:jaxb-api dependency provides the missing JAXB classes required during compilation on JDK 11, resolving the fatal compile error and allowing the Lombok version bump and subsequent migration steps to proceed.

history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading Spring Boot to 2.7 first aligns the dependency tree with modern standards and resolves transitive classpath conflicts. Adding JAXB dependencies and upgrading the compiler plugin ensures the initial compilation succeeds before stepping up to Java 11, 17, and 21.
